text: Motivated by our recent discovery of synthesis of X B 3 C 3 (X= Sr, La) compounds in bipartite sodalite clathrate structures, we used density functional theory (DFT) to study the chemistry of this group of compounds including the derivatives of mixed metal composition. The DFT-chemical pressure analysis of Mg-B-C, Ca-B-C, Sr-B-C and Ba-B-C systems shows that the size of the divalent metal cation has large effects on the structural stability of X B 3 C 3 -type compounds and that an appropriate metal size is necessary for the structural stability of these compounds at ambient pressure. High-throughput screening (HTS) of 105 binary-guest XYB 6 C 6 compounds with Pm 3 ( Pm 3 ̄) symmetry found that 22 compounds are dynamically stable at 1 atm. The studies significantly expand the pool of possible synthesis compounds by 19 that are consisting of 18 different metals and 1 insulator. Importantly, the electronic density of states (DOS) at the Fermi level and the superconducting transition temperature (T c ) in XYB 6 C 6 compounds can be controlled within certain ranges by adjusting the average valence of the mixed metal composition and the T c maximizes at average valence of +1.5. The highest predicted Eliashberg T c is 88 K at 1 atm found for KPB6C6. Thus, KPBB6C6 seems to be an optimal candidate which might be synthesized by high pressure and high temperature and then quenched down to ambient pressure.
